Article content: {"cover_media":"3_2031956667881967616","preview_text":"YC's motto is "Make Something People Want."
Time to print a second t-shirt.
Agents are choosing tools now. Not humans browsing Product Hunt, AI agents reading documentation and picking winners in real","plain_text":"YC's motto is "Make Something People Want."
Time to print a second t-shirt.
Agents are choosing tools now. Not humans browsing Product Hunt, AI agents reading documentation and picking winners in real time.
One email API found that ChatGPT became a top 3 acquisition channel. Not Google. Not ads. An AI recommending them because their docs were simply good.
The legacy competitor? A customer support maze. No code snippets. In a world where agents recommend, that's not bad UX .  it's invisibility.
Here's what's changing:
Agents don't browse websites. They call APIs.Agents don't sign up for subscriptions. They pay per request.Agents don't ask friends. They read docs.
Now imagine: an agent hits your API, gets a 402  Payment Required. Not an error. An invitation. It reads the terms, signs a tx, gets the data. No accounts. No dashboards. Value exchanged at the speed of a network request.

That's the agent economy. Not SaaS pricing pages for human psychology, protocol-native micropayments for autonomous software.
The builders who win from here will:
- Build APIs first, websites second
- Treat docs as their #1 go-to-market channel
- Design payments agents can complete without a human
- Go open, agents love open source, hate walled gardens
The developer market just went from 20M engineers to hundreds of millions of anyone-with-a-laptop builders, plus all their agents making decisions alongside them.
The agent economy isn't coming. It's already here, choosing tools while most of us sleep.

Make something agents want.
---
*Inspired by YC's Lightcone podcast*","title":"Make Something Agents Want"}
